description: Files exempt from Phase 7 lint rules. Permanent entries document WHY the file is structurally exempt. Temporary entries track active pipeline scripts with known violations awaiting Phase B mop-up — remove each entry as the violation is fixed. Active pipeline scripts are NOT eligible for permanent amnesty.

loop-query.note: Ban: pool.query() inside for/for-of/forEach/.map() loops (N+1 queries)

permanent.reason: SDK implementation — pool.query in loops is the internal retry/batch mechanism
Utility lib — bounded loop over config keys, not row-level data
Orchestrator — per-step pool.query UPDATEs are bounded (≤24 steps)
Migration runner — one query per migration file, not a pipeline step
Seed script — inert, not in active chain
Seed script — inert, not in active chain
One-off maintenance script — not in active chain
Generator tool — uses no pool.query at all
Task scaffolder — uses no pool.query at all
Spec audit tool — uses no pool.query
O(neighbourhoods) not O(permits): one UNNEST batch UPDATE per neighbourhood (≤158 max), each handling many permits via array params — bounded by geography, not data volume

temporary.reason: Phase B mop-up: upsertBatch N+1 being refactored to multi-row VALUES (WF3 Phase 3)
Phase B mop-up: 2 loop queries being refactored to UNNEST batch DELETE + spatial fallback (WF3 Phase 3)

permanent.reason: SDK — implements withTransaction itself; internal mutations are the implementation
Orchestrator — pipeline_runs bookkeeping is intentionally outside transaction (observation records)
Migration runner — DDL cannot run inside a transaction
Seed script — inert, not in active chain
Seed script — inert, not in active chain
Seed script — inert, not in active chain
One-time backfill — inert, not in active chain
One-off maintenance script — not in active chain
CQA script — pipeline_runs INSERT is observation time recording, not data mutation
CQA script — pipeline_runs INSERT is observation time recording
CQA script — engine_health_snapshots INSERT is observation time recording
CQA script — read-only probe
CQA script — read-only probe
CQA script — read-only probe
CQA script — read-only probe
CQA script — read-only probe
Batched DELETE is idempotent and crash-safe; each batch is independent

new-date-ban.note: Ban: new Date() in scripts/ for DB timestamp generation (use pipeline.getDbTimestamp instead)

permanent.reason: SDK — uses new Date().toISOString() for snapshot captured_at (observation metadata, not data timestamp)
Uses new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10) to compare expiry dates as strings (date comparison, not DB write)
Report generator — uses new Date() for report date in file headers
Generator tool — uses new Date() for template comments
Task scaffolder — uses new Date() for date stamps in task files
Migration runner — uses Date for logging only
Orchestrator — uses Date for chain start/end logging only

sql-now.note: Ban: NOW() or CURRENT_DATE inside mutation SQL (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) — use RUN_AT parameter instead

permanent.reason: SDK — checkQueueAge uses NOW() in WHERE clause (read-only filter, not write)
Orchestrator — pipeline_runs timestamps are observation time (when chain ran), not data timestamps
Migration runner — DDL DEFAULT NOW() is appropriate in schema definitions
All mutations are inside REPEATABLE READ transaction — NOW() is stable for the transaction duration
CQA script — pipeline_runs NOW() records observation time, not data timestamps
CQA script — pipeline_runs NOW() records observation time
CQA script — observation time recording
CQA script — NOW() in WHERE clause (read-only age filter)
CQA script — read-only probe
CQA script — NOW() in WHERE clause
CQA script — NOW() in WHERE clause
CQA script — read-only probe
Seed script — inert, not in active chain
One-time backfill — inert
CURRENT_DATE in WHERE clause only (age filter, not a write)
NOW() in WHERE clause only (freshness filter, not a write)
NOW() in WHERE clause only (age filter for DELETE boundary)
One-off maintenance — not in active chain
NOW() in WHERE clause only (permit expiry age filter, not a write)
NOW() in WHERE clauses only (age filters, not writes); RUN_AT used for SET clauses
NOW() in WHERE clause only (last_seen_at < NOW() - 1s freshness guard, not a write)
pipeline_runs NOW() records observation time (when script ran), not a data timestamp
pipeline_runs NOW() records observation time (when script ran), not a data timestamp
pipeline_runs NOW() records observation time (when script ran), not a data timestamp
sync_runs NOW() records observation time (when sync ran), not a data timestamp
NOW() appears in code comments only (documenting what the code does NOT do); all SET clauses use RUN_AT
NOW() in SELECT COUNT FILTER only (read-only aggregation reporting upcoming decisions, not a write)
NOW() appears in code comment only (documenting what load-permits does); all SET clauses use RUN_AT
NOW() appears in code comments only; all SET clauses use RUN_AT
NOW() appears in comments only (documenting the anti-pattern avoided); batch UPSERT uses RUN_AT
NOW() appears in comment only (documenting Midnight Cross prevention); UPSERT uses RUN_AT
NOW() in SELECT-only query (captures run_at + today for forecast logic, not in a SET clause)

unbounded-push-in-stream.note: Ban: .push() into outer array inside for-await without batch-flush guard (OOM risk)

permanent.reason: SDK — streamQuery implementation uses bounded internal buffers
Phase B mop-up: known accumulation pattern; tracked in review_followups.md B2

raw-parseint.note: Ban: raw parseInt()/parseFloat() in scripts/ without safe-math wrappers (NaN propagation risk)

permanent.reason: SDK — parseInt for port parsing is validated before use
Utility lib — config values validated via Zod after parsing
Safe-math implementation — uses parseInt internally with guards
Migration runner — not a pipeline step
Spec audit tool
Generator tool
Task scaffolder
Seed script — inert
Seed script — inert
One-off maintenance — inert
One-time backfill — inert
Orchestrator — parseInt for config (bounded, validated)
